name: Random Two-Decimal Percentage
short_description: Generates a random percentage value between 10% and 50%, rounded to two decimal places, to be used as a float.

name: Format Money
short_description: Formats a numerical value as a currency string with two decimal places (USD).

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: The numerical value to be formatted.

name: Format Decimal as Percentage
short_description: Converts a decimal value into its percentage equivalent. For instance, 0.05 becomes the numerical value 5, which is then typically displayed as '5.00%'.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: The decimal number to be converted to a percentage (e.g., 0.05 for 5%).

name: Simple Budget Allocation
short_description: Splits a quarterly budget between Marketing (given percentage) and Operations (remainder).

inputs.name: total_budget
inputs.description: The total quarterly budget available in currency units.

inputs.name: marketing_percentage
inputs.description: The percentage of the total budget to allocate to Marketing (e.g., enter 25 for 25%).

name: Budget Reallocation: Marketing to Operations
short_description: Calculates new Marketing and Operations budgets after a specified percentage of the Marketing budget is reallocated to Operations.

inputs.name: marketing_budget
inputs.description: The initial budget allocated to Marketing.

inputs.name: operations_budget
inputs.description: The initial budget allocated to Operations.

inputs.name: reallocation_percentage
inputs.description: The percentage of the Marketing budget to be reallocated to Operations (e.g., 15 for 15%).

name: Budget Adjustment for New Project
short_description: Allocates a total budget to R&D, Marketing, and Operations based on initial percentages, then adjusts R&D and Operations budgets by reallocating an additional amount to R&D.

inputs.name: total_budget
inputs.description: The total budget available for allocation (e.g., quarterly budget).

inputs.name: r_and_d_percentage
inputs.description: The initial percentage of the total budget allocated to Research & Development.

inputs.name: marketing_percentage
inputs.description: The initial percentage of the total budget allocated to Marketing.

inputs.name: additional_r_and_d
inputs.description: The additional amount to be allocated to R&D, which will be funded by reducing the Operations budget.

name: Budget Cut Reforecast
short_description: Calculates new budget allocations for R&D, Marketing, and Operations after a percentage cut to the total quarterly budget, maintaining original allocation percentages.

inputs.name: total_budget
inputs.description: Initial total quarterly budget.

inputs.name: r_and_d_pct
inputs.description: Initial percentage of total budget allocated to R&D.

inputs.name: marketing_pct
inputs.description: Initial percentage of total budget allocated to Marketing.

inputs.name: cut_pct
inputs.description: Percentage reduction applied to the total budget.

name: Long-Term Quarterly Budget Allocation
short_description: Projects a quarterly budget's growth over several years with an annual compound rate and allocates it into R&D, Marketing, and Operations categories.

inputs.name: current_quarterly_budget
inputs.description: The current total quarterly budget.

inputs.name: annual_growth_rate
inputs.description: The annual compound growth rate of the budget (as a percentage, e.g., 5 for 5%).

inputs.name: r_and_d_percentage
inputs.description: The percentage of the future quarterly budget allocated to R&D (e.g., 15 for 15%).

inputs.name: marketing_percentage
inputs.description: The percentage of the future quarterly budget allocated to Marketing (e.g., 20 for 20%).

inputs.name: number_of_years
inputs.description: The number of years for the long-term projection horizon.

name: Financial Template Problem Generator
short_description: A utility function that generates multiple instances of various financial problem templates with different random seeds and saves them to a specified JSON file.

inputs.name: output_file
inputs.description: The file path where the generated financial problems (questions and solutions) will be saved in JSON format.

inputs.name: num_instances
inputs.description: The number of problem instances to generate for each financial template function listed within the script.
